mechanism_narrative: Rising real savings indicate increased household balance sheet strength, which translates into higher equity demand and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rising RSAFS periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid decline in the unemployment rate below 5.0% signals tight labor market conditions that boost consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are negative or flat in more than 60% of instances where UNRATE drops below 5.0%, or predictive Rank IC falls below 0.04.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in housing starts and nonfarm payrolls reflects synchronized expansion in construction and broader employment, amplifying aggregate demand and yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of historical co-occurrences, or partial correlation with SPX returns drops below 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: In a monetary tightening cycle, rising real policy rates relative to inflation compress equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive or statistically indistinguishable from zero during periods where FEDFUNDS growth exceeds CPIAUCSL growth by more than 0.5pp.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained increases in the industrial production index reflect expanding manufacturing capacity and output, which directly support corporate revenue growth and generate positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: Rolling 1-year Spearman correlation between monthly INDPRO changes and 5-day forward SPX returns remains below 0.02 across all test windows.

mechanism_narrative: When quarterly real GDP growth accelerates above 3.0% annualized, it signals robust economic momentum that lifts equity risk premiums and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of quarters where GDP growth surpasses 3.0%, or predictive Rank IC falls below 0.04.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising nonfarm payrolls and a stable or falling unemployment rate indicates high-quality job creation that sustains wage growth without triggering inflationary pressures, supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in predicting 60-day forward returns, or forward returns in divergence periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of disinflation, declining month-over-month CPI readings reduce uncertainty and lower long-term discount rates, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 60% of months where CPI declines, or the signal's information ratio drops below 0.5 over a 3-year window.
